title: Crispy Golden Canadian Russet Potato Galette
description: A sweet, flaky, and savory brunch dish featuring russet potato, shio-kiroji-cure, and a touch of maple syrup.
instructions: Peel and thinly slice the potatoes using a mandoline or sharp knife.
In a large bowl, combine sliced potatoes, maple syrup, and shio-kiroji cure. Toss to coat.
Let the potatoes sit for 30 minutes to allow the cure to take effect.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the butter to a 1/4-inch thickness.
Place the egg in a small bowl and beat gently. Brush the edges of the butter with the egg.
Layer the potato slices on top of the butter, slightly overlapping each other.
Sprinkle with salt and fresh thyme. Fold the butter over the potatoes to form a galette.
Place the galette on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue baking for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
